messages.text: The C6 cross conveyor isn't in the quick view, but we’ve already cleaned it; we are waiting for the allergen test results.
we installed them before we left it was verified with QC
What about tunnel 2? QA is reporting that we don't have filters for that.
Elizabeth Hernandez I have two pump sprayers if you wanna send somebody over for them
Thank you
I told the lead they can use these too
The C6 cross conveyor has already passed the allergen test.
Tunnel 2 was left without a filter.
We flushed the oil from MT1 to BT1 and doing righ now MT2 to BT2.
Started to fill MT1 with Chocolate 10294
Belt on separator table have some damage, I don't know if was like that before
MD is in place S2

We did all the flushes we were supposed to do, I got in touch with Erandy to see if we should start filling the MT1 with the white chocolate and it was started, it melts very slowly, we're going to fill it as much as we can without causing a clog.
Buffet tank 6
The chocolate we put in the MT1 is already hot but it doesn't melt completely, it stays on the top.
CL limits read it should be 45-51
Yes
Maintenance is working on it but is there a limit to when we want to stop? Or is it okay as long as temper is in range
The decrystalizer is heating the chocolate and the chocolate is keeping the tank warm. If we spike up again we can raise pre-treat
Understood thanks for your response!
Maintenance increased set point and it’s been slowly increasing
As long as the mass pump keeps circulation then the tank will stay warm but it definitely makes it harder to keep temper, but the temper tests will tell us when we're in trouble.
Glyco normal
Startup checklist
Metal Detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly.

Temperature is back to normal. Once that started going back up we lowered mixing water actively to help balance temper. 

Last temper was 0.0
Throughout the day, the line has operated without difficulties or deviations. No incident reports or negative findings have been issued by the Quality Department.
We’ve been getting temper around .2/.3 so we’ve been lowering it. It’s still within ideal range.
Metal Detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly.
Chad Stone I just wanted to confirm. The schedule reads for 3rd shift to run for 3.8hrs is that still the plan?
During the inspection of the belts, the Quality Department observed damage in the demolding area; both the line leader and the supervisor were informed.

I’ve looked at it and it looks like it’s from where the connect the belts and has rubbed off. I don’t see any loose pieces. I don’t believe we have maintenance currently so I can do a follow up action for 3rd shift to have their work on it while they clean tonight
They will run 3 and clean for 5
Is this the demolding belt at the discharge of the Imperial?
Yes
Maintenance is here and looking now

That belt was just changed a couple weeks ago after the almond run. 

Preston Elmer, if that is the case then I think there may have been an issue with install.
Glyco normal

Thanks Lorena for informing the team. 

Nathan reached out to ask whether there was a potential quality issue and if the line needed to be stopped. My recommendation is to continue monitoring the belt. As long as there is no loose belt material present, the belt replacement can be wait until the scheduled cleaning.
Preston Tregaskis Kevin Del Valle for visibility. 

Lorena Abreu can you help ops monitor this issue please?
Sorry we decided to stop and try to trim it real quick
Thank you for the update. That sounds like a good plan, keep us posted if anything changes.
Maintenance did a good job. It turned out really well.
I'll get pictures monday morning and get an email out to C&E. 😡
Fw running well zero open
Adjustment 0.2 made on the mixing water at 10:38.

Update from third shift: Product meets all specifications. Bar weight and depth dimensions are at target values. Lot placement for film has been correctly. Packaging is following all specifications. Correct lot is printed on both the display/film. Silver part on the back of the film is below 3mm. The film packaging is centered. Production is using the correct shipping box label. 
A master case was checked and we have 0% of the open bars.
MD and C6 are running correctly

Flow wrap check


Zero open
We finished running. EEs go to lunch. Waiting for maintenance to start disassemble.

Maintenance start disassemble 





Level 2 clean in process
Level 2 clean in process
Countdown

Time tracker for cleaning 


It stay on the whiteboard
Maintenance removed C2 Belt and they are going to replace it.
Dorner 1 is damaged, will need to be fully inspected Monday if replacement is required.
Shut down
We don't replace that belt Preston Tregaskis it is spliced in by Conveyors & Equipment.

Rollers transfer tables 1&2
Rollers transfer tables 1&2

We noticed that the Tunnel 1 belt wasn't tracking properly, so we called maintenance to see if they could adjust it. We made an improvement, but the belt is still favoring the West side. We believe that dirty rollers could be affecting it, and should be cleaned when production stops, but for now we can continue to run as scheduled. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Caramel cook times

MM5 Centerline Yamato and Matrix also, the arms are intact

MM3 Pouch check 


Weight = 795g

Doubles = 94g - 11.82%
In my opinion, we need to stop cooling tunnel #1 and clean the rollers, the belt will likely favor the west side otherwise

Weight = 803


Pieces = 33


Doubles = 0
Follow up about the missing scraper?

I asked Nathan Gevenois to follow up with maintenance. 

Kimberly Morales said it was missing and I wanted to see if maintenance has an idea of spares or how to solve.
Im with him now looking at it
Sorry this got lost in the fray and wasn't followed up on back then. This exact scraper has been missing since April 3rd.
We don't have spares but I will get some on the way as soon as possible. Chad Stone
Thank you.

I want to congratulate all of the ML4 team on the HER Salted Caramel 13.3g launch! This was the first new item on ML4 where every single goal was met by the last day of production launch! OEE, scrap, DT, and HC goals met! Good work and excellent execution! Please share with your team and continue to drive excellence!Rose Smuin Chad Stone Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Alan Greer
When WH tries to receive the oreos in the RF-smart system, it gives an error message, "This order is being received by someone else".

Benjamin Hicks,

Can you follow up on Monday?

Katya Farnsworth, FYI
The cardboard vendor dropped we need to get with  international paper and the driver that hauled for them
